titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5968

div.p: To all Christ's faithful to whom the present written document shall come, Hugh Levedyman of Langehope, and Eve his wife, sister of Laurence son of William son of Gocelin of Berwick, send greeting. Know us, all of you, at the urging of the said Laurence, to have granted, and by this our present charter to have made firm, quitclaimed, and for ourselves and our heirs entirely to have abjured for ever the donation and grant of the whole land without any withholding in the territory of Prenderguest which Laurence, son of William son of Gocelin of Berwick, had from the gift of Robert his brother, which the same Laurence gave and granted to the lord Richard the prior, and the convent of Coldingham, in return for a certain sum of money paid to him in advance on account of his great need. We have pledged and sworn, touching holy relics, for ourselves and our heirs, that we shall in no era of the world seek to move against the aforesaid donation and grant. As witness of this matter, we have applied our seals to the present document. As witnesses, the lord William of Mordington, the lord David of Haddington, the lord Ralph Noble, the lord William of Scremerston, Adam of Prenderguest, Adam son of John of Over Ayton, Thomas of Nisbet, Bertram of Reston, Patrick Scott, Gilbert of Lumsdaine, John son of Ingram, Gregory of Bethock, and others.
